headline.conditional_law: Reasoning-time overconfidence under knowledge conflict is best written as a conditional RLVR / misspecification law, not as a universal scale law.

recommended_statement: Models trained with verifiable-reward reasoning objectives can enter a misspecified, endogenous-evidence regime under knowledge conflict, where longer CoT sharpens confidence faster than it improves accuracy. The effect is benchmark-dependent and strongest on controlled conflict families.

contrast_points.result: CoT improves confidence calibration in most settings.
contrast_points.our_read: Compatible. That is the well-specified or low-conflict side of the split.
Compatible. This is the hard-QA / misspecified side of the split.
This is the best mechanistic phrase for the endogenous-evidence side of theorem 3.

contrast_points.result: Binary reward reasoning hurts calibration; Brier-style rewards repair it.
contrast_points.our_read: This is the training-time cousin of our test-time conditional law.
